name: Carbon and sustainability
existence_evidence: GreenCache (Cache Your Prompt When It's Green): operational carbon down from avoided compute; embodied carbon from SSD cache storage; 12-25% net carbon reduction in evaluated scenarios with adaptive sizing.
gap_vs_vision: No mainstream gateway exposes carbon-aware cache sizing; org should track tokens avoided not only dollars.
summary: Caching reduces inference energy; large persistent KV/semantic stores add storage manufacturing emissions—net benefit depends on hit rate, cache size, grid carbon intensity.
how_it_works: GreenCache resizes cache based on load and carbon intensity (CI); high CI favors larger cache; low CI favors smaller cache to limit embodied cost.
integration_points: Platform SRE, sustainability reporting, LMCache storage tier choices.
correctness_risks: [uncertain] exact net carbon for semantic Redis vs GPU savings without org-specific measurement.
cost_latency_impact: Aligned with cost savings—fewer tokens computed.
environmental_impact: Cross-user caching multiplies avoided compute per stored entry; measure tokens_saved and estimate kg CO2e via grid factors.
cross_user_sharing: org_wide_with_guards — more users per entry improves carbon amortization
pii_and_compliance_controls: N/A to carbon; don't skip compliance for green claims.
org_scale_patterns: Report cache hit rate alongside estimated emissions avoided in ESG dashboards.
makes_sense_rationale: Supports business case beyond cost; quantify after deployment.
